name: Hadoop 2.x Architecture (YARN & HDFS)
chatResponse: I will guide you through the architectural shift of Hadoop 2.x, specifically how YARN serves as the cluster's operating system to decouple resource management from data processing.

steps.title: Decoupling with YARN
steps.content: Unlike Hadoop 1.x where MapReduce handled both resource management and data processing, Hadoop 2.x introduces **YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator)**. This allows Hadoop to support multiple processing engines like Spark, Storm, and Giraph alongside traditional MapReduce. The architecture is split into two main layers: **HDFS** for storage and **YARN** for resource allocation.

steps.title: The Storage Layer (HDFS)
steps.content:
HDFS remains the primary storage system. It consists of:
- **NameNode**: The master that manages metadata (file-to-block mapping).
- **DataNodes**: Slaves that store actual data blocks and perform read/write operations as directed by the NameNode.

steps.title: The Resource Management Layer (YARN)
steps.content:
YARN introduces a hierarchical management system:
- **ResourceManager (RM)**: The ultimate authority that arbitrates resources among all competing applications.
- **NodeManager (NM)**: The per-machine agent responsible for monitoring resource usage (CPU, memory) and reporting to the RM.

steps.title: Application Execution Workflow
steps.content:
When a job is submitted:
1. The **ResourceManager** allocates a container to start the **ApplicationMaster (AM)**.
2. The AM negotiates appropriate resource containers from the RM.
3. The AM then coordinates with **NodeManagers** to execute and monitor specific tasks within those containers.

visualCode:
graph TD
    subgraph Client_Space [Client Interface]
        User[Client Application]
    end

    subgraph YARN_Cluster [YARN - Resource Layer]
        RM[Resource Manager]
        RM -.-> |Allocates| AM[Application Master]
        RM --- NM1[Node Manager 1]
        RM --- NM2[Node Manager 2]
    end

    subgraph HDFS_Cluster [HDFS - Storage Layer]
        NN[NameNode]
        NN --- DN1[DataNode 1]
        NN --- DN2[DataNode 2]
    end

    subgraph Execution_Env [Execution Context]
        NM1 --> C1[Container 1: Task]
        NM2 --> C2[Container 2: Task]
        AM --> |Manages| C1
        AM --> |Manages| C2
    end

    User --> RM
    User --> NN
    DN1 --- NM1
    DN2 --- NM2

    style RM fill:#1e293b,stroke:#0ea5e9,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    style NN fill:#1e293b,stroke:#f43f5e,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    style AM fill:#334155,stroke:#10b981,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    style Default fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
